steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s conclusion is that carbon dioxide does not serve as an additional cue for midges. The decisive clue is the comparison between the two traps: if CO₂ has no added value, the trap with calls alone should perform as well as or better than the combined trap.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice C shows that the calls-only trap attracted more midges than the combined one, which directly supports the conclusion that CO₂ adds no benefit. The other three either show CO₂ helping midges (A, B, and D), contradicting the conclusion. That matches choice C — C is correct.
